title: Street Talk: Bupa in $450m deal to buy Partnered Health
full_text:
Bupa is making its biggest push into allied and holistic healthcare in Australia after shelling out about $450 million to buy private-equity-backed medical centres group Partnered Health.
Sources briefed on the deal said the British health insurance giant signed on the dotted line on Wednesday night.
The deal detailing the acquisition of 60-plus medical and skin cancer clinics, occupational health sites, and allied health networks was confirmed about noon on Thursday Sydney time.
The move is in line with Bupa's stated objective to scale its primary health care offering and provide broader services, including mental health and allied health, to prevent, diagnose and treat conditions and illnesses.
The group already has a range of medical centres across NSW, Queensland and Victoria and a handful of the federal government's Medicare Urgent Care Clinics. It also offers dental, optical and hearing services, mental health clinics and telehealth consultations.
Last year, the British-based group said it planned to open 200 mental health clinics globally, with Australia making up more than a quarter of the rollout.
It bought Eastbrooke Medical Centres and its 13 outposts in January 2025, muscling up to Medibank, which snapped up Better Medical, a network of 61 medical clinics, in November, and boosted its investment in Myhealth Medical Group in 2023.
Quadrant bought Partnered Health, one of the country's largest integrated healthcare providers, from Singapore's Fullerton Healthcare in 2020.
Managing partner Gareth Woodbridge and investment director Tyson Smith oversaw the investment and split the company into two units: primary care and corporate health and wellbeing.
It was making about$40 million-plus in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation when corporate advisory firm Stanton Road Partners was hired earlier this year to find a buyer.
Quadrant had Stanton Road principal Peter Brownie and King & Wood Mallesons' partner Matthew Coull on the tools. Morgan Stanley healthcare banker Rowan Bailey and Ashurst legal eagle Anton Harris acted for Bupa.
Fairfax Media Management Pty Limited
author: Sarah Thompson, Kanika Sood and Emm
heading: Health and Disability Insurance
